name: Common bean (food medium)
basis: per 100 g raw mature dry seed (Phaseolus vulgaris)

components.name: water
components.evidence: Beans, kidney, red, mature seeds, raw; dry seed basis.

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Dominant nitrogen source; storage globulin (phaseolin) rich seed.

components.name: starch
components.source: Punia et al. 2020 Legume Science doi:10.1002/leg3.52; by-difference from USDA FDC 173744 (carb 61.3 - fiber 15.2 - sugars)
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source. Bean seed starch typically 40-45 g/100g (flour 39.7-43.8%); high-amylose (~30-45%).

components.name: dietary fiber (total)
components.evidence: Includes resistant starch, cellulose, pectin; largely non-fermentable by non-specialist microbes.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Foods 2025 15(2):391 doi:10.3390/foods15020391 (PMC12841103), 12 P. vulgaris accessions HPLC-RI
components.evidence: Predominant free sugar (25.7 +/- 5.1 mg/g). USDA 'total sugars' = 2.1 g/100g is consistent.

components.name: stachyose
components.evidence: Co-dominant soluble carbohydrate (26.0 +/- 4.9 mg/g); alpha-galactoside, key fermentable oligosaccharide. Range wide across studies (0.13-2.6 g/100g; red haricot 1.26 g/100g, PMC7439167).

components.name: raffinose
components.evidence: 2.5 mg/g (range 0.8-7.4). alpha-galactoside; fermentable by RFO-utilizing microbes.

components.name: verbascose
components.evidence: 0.8 mg/g (range 0.3-1.3); minor in P. vulgaris (major in peas).

components.name: myo-inositol
components.evidence: 0.6 mg/g free cyclitol; also bound as phytate.

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence: 0.4 mg/g, detected in ~half of accessions; released from alpha-galactosides.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Trace only in raw dry seed (detected in 1/12 accessions); free monosaccharides are minimal in mature dry beans.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Not detected in any of 12 raw dry-seed accessions; present only after hydrolysis/germination.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Not detected as a free sugar in raw dry seed; appears on starch hydrolysis. Included as null (FooDB lists it).

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Massey et al. 2001 J Agric Food Chem doi:10.1021/jf010484y; oxalatecontent.com (kidney bean raw)
components.evidence: Total oxalate ~40 mg/100g in raw kidney bean; soluble fraction lower (~1.5-1.8 mg/100g, PMC10599953). Low-oxalate legume.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Green-bean LC method PMID:8069112; Phaseolus seed metabolomics PMC9405002 / PMC10820859
components.evidence: Confirmed present as a main organic acid of P. vulgaris; no reliable per-100g quantification for raw dry seed located. Included with null (present, unquantified).

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Green-bean LC method PMID:8069112; Sustainability 2021 doi:10.3390/su13126869; Phaseolus seed metabolomics PMC9405002
components.evidence: Confirmed present, typically the largest organic acid in P. vulgaris tissue; not reliably quantified per-100g in raw dry seed. Null (present, unquantified).

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Detected in trace amounts. Null (present, unquantified).

components.name: phytic acid
components.evidence: myo-Inositol hexaphosphate; major P and cation chelator in seed. Reported here (was in uncovered).

components.name: phosphorus
components.evidence: P; a large share is phytate-bound.

components.name: vitamin C (ascorbate)
components.evidence: Modest in dry seed; degrades on storage/cooking.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: Food folate (DFE 394 ug); beans are a leading dietary source.

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: Low; gamma-tocopherol is the main bean tocopherol but not separately in this record.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Protein-bound AA profile of raw kidney bean.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Predominant PUFA of common bean seed.

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid soil contaminant; not a nutrient

remove.name: Vanadium
remove.reason: ultratrace element, not an established growth nutrient here (FooDB noise)

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element tracking K (FooDB noise)

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed vitamin A is animal-derived; USDA reports 0 in dry bean; implausible in a plant seed

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae (parsnip/celery); FooDB mis-assignment, implausible in Phaseolus

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: microbial-fermentation food preservative; not a native bean metabolite

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: non-physiological diastereomer / duplicate artifact of isoleucine

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: trace flavor volatile, not a quantifiable growth nutrient

missing_added: individual sugars with amounts: sucrose, stachyose, raffinose, verbascose, D-galactose, myo-inositol
starch quantified (~40 g/100g) as the dominant carbon source
full USDA quantification of all minerals, vitamins, amino acids and fatty acids
keep_uncovered: Phytic acid metabolite class detail (kept as component)
Pelargonidin (genuine seed-coat anthocyanins in colored beans)
Silicon (ultratrace, plausibly present; no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 173744 (Beans, kidney, red, mature seeds, raw)
Foods 2025 15(2):391 doi:10.3390/foods15020391 (PMC12841103) - soluble sugars/oligosaccharides in 12 P. vulgaris accessions (HPLC-RI)
PMC7439167 - raw red haricot bean oligosaccharides and phytic acid
Punia et al. 2020 Legume Science doi:10.1002/leg3.52 - kidney bean starch review
Massey et al. 2001 J Agric Food Chem doi:10.1021/jf010484y; oxalatecontent.com - legume oxalate content
PMID:8069112 - LC organic acids (oxalic/malic/citric/fumaric) in Phaseolus vulgaris
Sustainability 2021 doi:10.3390/su13126869; PMC9405002; PMC10820859 - Phaseolus seed organic-acid presence
notes: Curated as the raw mature dry seed (Phaseolus vulgaris). Dominant carbon/energy source is STARCH (~40 g/100g); the growth-relevant free carbohydrate pool is sucrose (~2.6) plus the RFO oligosaccharides stachyose (~2.6) and raffinose (~0.25) - free glucose/fructose are essentially absent in the dry seed. Nitrogen is supplied chiefly by ~22.5 g/100g protein (Glu/Asp/Lys-rich). Organic acids are minor here (oxalic ~40 mg/100g; malic/citric/fumaric present but not reliably quantified in dry seed) - unlike fruits, they are not a major carbon source. Removed toxic/non-nutritive trace metals and biologically implausible FooDB predicted compounds; retained genuine seed-coat phenolics/anthocyanins as uncovered.
